steps.title: Write price in terms of units sold
steps.content: Let $n$ be the number of products sold. The price starts at $96$ dollars when $0$ products are sold, then drops by $1.50$ for each additional product sold, so $$\text{price}=96-1.5n.$$

steps.title: Set up the revenue equation
steps.content: Revenue equals price times quantity: $$(96-1.5n)n=1522.50.$$ Expanding and rearranging gives $$96n-1.5n^2=1522.50,$$ $$1.5n^2-96n+1522.50=0.$$ Divide by $1.5$: $$n^2-64n+1015=0.$$

steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content: Use the quadratic formula or factor by the roots. The discriminant is $$64^2-4(1015)=4096-4060=36.$$ Thus $$n=\frac{64\pm 6}{2},$$ giving $n=35$ or $n=29$.

steps.title: Match the answer choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $29$: one valid solution, so this could be the number sold. ✓
  • Choice B — $32$: gives revenue $32(96-48)=1,536$, not $1,522.50$. ✗
  • Choice C — $951$: far exceeds the zero-price point at $n=64$, so it is impossible in this model. ✗
  • Choice D — $1,536$: also far exceeds the zero-price point. ✗
The other mathematical root, $35$, is not listed, so A is correct.
